questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In a cubic unit cell, atoms (A) are present at all corners, (B) are present at alternate face centre and all edge centres. Atoms (C) are present at face centres left from (B) and one at each body diagonal at distance of $1 / 4^{\text{th }}$ of body diagonal from corner.Question: Formula of given solid is
questions.solution:
Atoms $\mathrm{A}($ at corner $)=8 \times \frac{1}{8}=1$
Atoms $B=2$ (alternate face centre) $\times \frac{1}{2}$
$+12(\text { edge centre }) \times \frac{1}{4}=4$

Atoms $C=4$ (face centre left from B) $\times \frac{1}{2}$
$+1 \times 4(\text { body diagonal })=6$
$\therefore$ Formula $\mathrm{AB}_4 \mathrm{C}_6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A solid crystal is composed of $X, Y$ and $Z$ atoms, $Z$ atoms (radius 200 pm ) form cubic close packed structure, whereas $X$ (radius 50 pm ) and $Y$ (radius 100 pm ) occupy respective voids - Assume all the voids are occupied. Now answer the following questions.Question: If all the atoms along one body diagonal are removed, then the formula of the compound becomes :
questions.solution:
In the original compound
$Z$ atoms $\rightarrow$ form CCP
X atoms $\rightarrow$ present in tetrahedral void.
$\left(\frac{r X}{r Z}=\frac{50}{200}=0.25\right)$
Y atoms $\rightarrow$ present in octahedral void
$\left(\frac{r Y}{r Z}=\frac{100}{200}=0.5\right)$
Assuming, all voids are occupied, formula of the original compound $=X_8 Y_4 Z_4$.
On removing atoms along one body diagonal,
We lose $\rightarrow 2 Z$ atoms, $2 X$ atoms, $1 Y$ atom (body centre)
$\therefore$ The new formula becomes $\rightarrow \mathrm{X}_6 \mathrm{Y}_3 \mathrm{Z}_{3.75}$
Simplest formula $\rightarrow \mathrm{X}_8 \mathrm{Y}_4 \mathrm{Z}_5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A solid crystal is composed of $X, Y$ and $Z$ atoms, $Z$ atoms (radius 200 pm ) form cubic close packed structure, whereas $X$ (radius 50 pm ) and $Y$ (radius 100 pm ) occupy respective voids - Assume all the voids are occupied. Now answer the following questions.Question: Now in the crystal obtained in Q. No. 13 if all the atoms along another body diagonal are removed, then the formula of the compound becomes :
questions.solution:
On removing atoms along another body diagonal, we lose
$\rightarrow 2 \mathrm{Z}$ atoms
$\rightarrow 2 \mathrm{X}$ atoms
$\therefore$ The new formula becomes $=\mathrm{X}_4 \mathrm{Y}_3 \mathrm{Z}_{3.5}$
Simplest formula $=X_B Y_6 Z_7$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In a cubic unit cell, atoms (A) are present at all corners, (B) are present at alternate face centre and all edge centres. Atoms (C) are present at face centres left from (B) and one at each body diagonal at distance of $1 / 4^{\text {th }}$ of body diagonal from corner.Question: An axis is passed from the given unit cell connecting opposite face centres and all atoms touching the axis are removed. The possible formula of compound left is
questions.solution:
If axis passes through face centre where $B$ lies, then

Number of B atoms $=4-\left(\frac{1}{2} \times 2\right)=3$
Formula $=\mathrm{AB}_3 \mathrm{C}_6$
If axis passes through face centres where C lies, then

Number of $C$ atoms $=6-\frac{1}{2} \times 2=5$
$\therefore$ Formula $=\mathrm{AB}_4 \mathrm{C}_5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
